figure.title: Tx Replay Queue Pointers
figure.purpose: Illustrates the pointers used to manage the Transmit (Tx) Replay Queue for error recovery.

technical_summary: Shows a Tx Replay buffer containing unacknowledged packets, labeled with sequence numbers (5, 6, 7, 8, 9).
Tx_last_seq points to the most recently transmitted sequence number (9).
Rx_last_ack points to the last sequence number successfully acknowledged by the receiver (4, which is now retired from the queue).
The range of sequence numbers between the two pointers defines the current window of outstanding requests/acknowledgments.

components.name: Tx Replay Queue
components.description: Buffer holding transmitted packets until they are acknowledged.

registers.name: Tx_last_seq
registers.description: Pointer to the sequence number of the last transmitted flit.

registers.name: Rx_last_ack
registers.description: Pointer to the sequence number of the last received acknowledgment.

critical_facts: The Replay Queue maintains a sliding window of transmitted packets to enable retransmission in case of errors, tracked by last sent and last acknowledged sequence pointers.
